_comment: construction-desk composes the residential-construction reference atoms. All lookups run offline against the scoop cache (canonical-sources/construction) and all math runs in tools/build_calc.py (no tokens, no copyrighted tables). Every output carries the verify-locally boundary from protocols/safety.md and shared/construction-engine.md; electrical/plumbing/structural/roofing/HVAC lead with the licensed-professional requirement. Reference and calculation only: no project planning (project-builder), no cost (finance-desk), no engineering or code-compliance advice.
description: Residential construction and DIY reference: dimensions and technique, code section and adopted edition (FL and NC), and first-principles calculators.

steps.input_from: the trade or topic in plain language
steps.condition: request is a construction_question (how-to, dimensions, common mistakes, glossary)
request is a code_lookup (governing section, adopted edition, free-viewer link)
request is a build_calc (stair, egress, R-value, box fill, drain slope, roof pitch, board foot, deck span)

steps.input_from: the assembled answer from steps 1 to 3
steps.condition: always; enforces the boundary, citations, no fabrication, and formatting before handoff

notes: A single request may fire more than one of steps 1 to 3 (for example a riser height needs build-calc plus code-lookup). project-builder calls construction-lookup, code-lookup, and build-calc directly for its materials list, step sequence, and safety notes.
